{{Quote|The rainbow bridge will appear and disappear. And the gods will make it seem that they are the authors of a great conflict. When the eclipse of the sun will then be, the divine omen will be seen in plain sight. Quite otherwise will one interpret it, for when a menacing stranger appears at the portal, look close at hand for the amphistaff that will send the stranger on its way.|The [[Shamed Ones|Shamed One]] [[Onimi]] recites a prophetic passage that [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] claimed to have heard|<ref name="Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>}}
The '''Rings of Coruscant''' referred to an artificially created [[Planetary ring/Legends|planetary ring]] that encircled the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The [[Yuuzhan Vong]] formed the ring, which they called '''the Bridge''' or the '''rainbow bridge''', in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]] after [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|capturing Coruscant]] and renaming it Yuuzhan'tar. The [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaders]] destroyed the planet's largest [[Moon/Legends|moon]] via [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulating [[dovin basal]]s and used the resulting debris to fashion a ring that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] Yuuzhan'tar. The resulting planetary ring appeared [[Color/Legends|rainbow]]-like from the planet surface. However, two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], the Bridge was heavily damaged when the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]-capable planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] arrived in Yuuzhan'tar's orbit, sundering the Bridge in the process. Even so, it remained in place around the planet a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] later, by which [[Time/Legends|time]] it was known as the Rings of Coruscant and was a popular tourist attraction.

==Description==
{{Quote|Magnificent, isn't it?|[[Vergere]], on the Bridge|<ref name="Traitor" />}}
The Rings of Coruscant—also known as the Bridge or the rainbow bridge—was an artificially created [[Planetary ring/Legends|planetary ring]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], renamed by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] as Yuuzhan'tar. Angled seventeen degrees away from the ecliptic of Coruscant, the ring consisted of dust, gravel, and lumps of hardened [[Magma/Legends|magma]]. Viewed from the surface of the world, the Rings of Coruscant took up almost a third of the [[Atmosphere/Legends|sky]] and appeared as a vibrant rainbow of [[Color/Legends|colors]] to those who look upon it; the light from the ring outclassed the reflected light from most [[Moon/Legends|moons]] at [[Standard day/Legends|night]].<ref name="Traitor" /> Coruscant's [[Coruscant Prime/Legends|sun]] illuminated the Rings of Coruscant during the day, making them appear [[Gemstone/Legends|jeweled]]-like.<ref name="Force" /> After the ring was hit by the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], much of the it was scattered, though it remained as a planetary ring around Coruscant.<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|93|Bridge, the (Rainbow Bridge)}}</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|The planet appeared out of darkspace and hurtled into the Yuuzhan'tar system, Fearsome One. It nearly grazed the holy world, sundering the rainbow bridge and scattering the moons—the innermost of which nearly struck Yuuzhan'tar as it was outward bound. It is a catastrophe of epic proportions, Warmaster.|[[Supreme Commander]] [[Saluup Fing]], to [[Warmaster]] [[Nas Choka]]|<ref name="Force" />}}
[[File:CoruscantRings-2006Legacy1.png|thumb|left|200px|The Rings of Coruscant as seen from Coruscant's surface a century after the Yuuzhan Vong War]]
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] Yuuzhan Vong [[Species/Legends|species]] invaded [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], engaging governments like the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]].<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> The invaders [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|captured]] the New Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet of Coruscant<ref name="Traitor" /> in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]].<ref name="ERC" /> Wanting to produce a replica of the destroyed Yuuzhan Vong [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Yuuzhan'tar]], the Vong began the process by obliterating Coruscant's largest [[Moon/Legends|moon]]<ref name="Traitor" /><ref name="Note">Although ''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]'' does not specify which of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] four [[Moon/Legends|moons]] was destroyed to create the Bridge, the destroyed moon must have been either [[Centax-1/Legends|Centax-1]] or [[Centax-3/Legends|Centax-3]], as the two remaining moons, [[Centax-2/Legends|Centax-2]] and [[Hesperidium/Legends|Hesperidium]], both appeared in stories dated after the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Centax-2 was featured in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Bloodlines]]'', and Hesperidium in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''.</ref> with the help of [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]-manipulating [[dovin basal]]s linked via the [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] capabilities of [[yammosk]] creatures. The Bridge was created out of the resulting debris as it was organized into a planetary ring by the dovin basals to mimic the ring that once orbited Yuuzhan'tar. With Coruscant [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] into an image of Yuuzhan'tar, the captured planet was renamed after the Yuuzhan Vong's homeworld.<ref name="Traitor" />

For the Yuuzhan Vong, who had taken the new Yuuzhan'tar as their home, the Bridge became symbolic of the communications between them and their high [[Yun'o|god]], [[Yun-Yuuzhan]]. However,<ref name="Force" /> in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]],<ref name="Unifying Force date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]].</ref> as the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]—the New Republic's successor state—and its allies mustered their forces to recapture Yuuzhan'tar, the sentient planet Zonama Sekot, capable of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel, made a hyperspace jump that landed it in Yuuzhan'tar's orbit,<ref name="Force" /> sundering much<ref name="TCSWE" /> of the Bridge in the process. After the breaking of the ring, with the Galactic Alliance [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|assaulting]] Yuuzhan'tar, [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] claimed to have heard a prophecy that foretold of the destruction of the Bridge before an eventual Yuuzhan Vong triumph and used this revelation to help spur on [[Gender/Legends|his]] forces in an attack on Zonama Sekot. Nevertheless, the Galactic Alliance defeated the Yuuzhan Vong, reclaimed Yuuzhan'tar, and renamed it Coruscant.<ref name="Force" />

Though the Bridge's integrity was compromised by the Zonama Sekot incident, the planetary ring was still in place around Coruscant<ref name="TCSWE" /> a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] later,<ref name="Broken">[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]]</ref> at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s reign.<ref name="TCSWE" /> By then, it had become known as the Rings of Coruscant and was a popular tourist destination on the surface despite the presence of wild [[Nexu/Legends|nexu]] creatures roaming near the planetary ruins.<ref name="Legacy">[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Rings of Coruscant first appeared, originally named the Bridge, in [[Matthew Stover]]'s [[The New Jedi Order: Traitor|''Traitor'']], a [[2002]] novel released as part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series. The planetary ring later appeared and was given the secondary name "rainbow bridge" in two successive volumes of ''The New Jedi Order'' series, [[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy|''The Final Prophecy'']] and [[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force|''The Unifying Force'']], written by [[Greg Keyes]] and [[James Luceno]], respectively, and released in [[2003]]. In [[2006]], the Bridge was identified as the Rings of Coruscant in [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]], the pre-release supplement of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, before appearing in some of the series' issues. The 2003 reference book ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' mistakenly states that all four of Coruscant's moons were destroyed to create the bridge.
